message:
fix(abi): move _Exit definition out of unistd_abi.rs (bd-sy6p6 follow-up)

The previous bd-sy6p6 commit added `_Exit` in process_abi.rs alongside `_exit`, but unistd_abi.rs still carried an older `frankenlibc_exit_immediate` wrapper exported under the same `_Exit` name. Two definitions of the same `extern "C" _Exit` symbol cause a duplicate-export error at link time.

* Drop `frankenlibc_exit_immediate` (export_name = "_Exit") from unistd_abi.rs (line 18304).
* Update the explanatory comment in the "_Exit / _Fork" section of unistd_abi.rs to point to the new home in process_abi.rs.
* Behavior change: the new definition forwards to `_exit` rather than calling `frankenlibc_core::syscall::sys_exit_group(status)` directly. That goes through our `_exit` path which clamps the status to the low 8 bits before issuing the exit_group syscall — the right thing per POSIX, and matches what the old `_exit`-using code expected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
